content:
# John Chapter 3 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/JHN_003_page_001.jpg, JHN_003_page_002.jpg

1. And there was a man of the Pharisaioi, Nikodemos [was] the name of him, a ruler of the Ioudaioi.

2. This [one] came to him by night and said to him, "Rabbi, we know that from God you have come [as] a teacher, for no one is able to do these signs that you do unless the God is with him."

3. The Yeshua answered and said to him, "Truly, truly I say to you, unless someone is born from above, he is not able to see the kingdom of the God."

4. Nikodemos says to him, "How is a man able to be born, being old? Is he able to enter into the womb of the mother of him a second [time] and be born?"

5. The Yeshua answered, "Truly, truly I say to you, unless someone is born of water and Spirit, he is not able to enter into the kingdom of the God.

6. The [thing] having been born of the flesh is flesh, and the [thing] having been born of the Spirit is spirit.

7. Do not marvel that I said to you, 'It is necessary for you to be born from above.'

8. The wind blows where it wills, and the sound of it you hear, but you do not know from where it comes and where it goes. So is everyone having been born of the Spirit."

9. Nikodemos answered and said to him, "How are these things able to be?"

10. The Yeshua answered and said to him, "You are the teacher of the Israel and you do not know these things?

11. Truly, truly I say to you, what we know we speak, and what we have seen we testify, and the testimony of us you do not receive.

12. If the earthly things I told you and you do not believe, how if I tell you the heavenly things will you believe?

13. And no one has ascended into the heaven except the [one] having descended from the heaven — the Son of the Man.

14. And just as Mouses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, so it is necessary [for] the Son of the Man to be lifted up,

15. that everyone believing in him may have eternal life.

16. For so the God loved the world that the Son, the only-begotten, he gave, that everyone believing in him may not perish but may have eternal life.

17. For the God did not send the Son into the world that he might judge the world, but that the world might be saved through him.

18. The [one] believing in him is not judged; the [one] not believing has already been judged, because he has not believed in the name of the only-begotten Son of the God.

19. And this is the judgment: that the light has come into the world, and the men loved the darkness rather than the light, for the works of them were evil.

20. For everyone practicing worthless [things] hates the light and does not come to the light, lest the works of him be exposed.

21. But the [one] doing the truth comes to the light, that the works of him may be revealed, that in God they have been worked."

22. After these things the Yeshua and the disciples of him came into the Ioudaian land, and there he was spending time with them and baptizing.

23. And Ioannes also was baptizing in Ainon near Saleim, because many waters were there, and [people] were coming and were being baptized.

24. For Ioannes had not yet been cast into the prison.

25. Then there arose a dispute from the disciples of Ioannes with a Ioudaios concerning purification.

26. And they came to Ioannes and said to him, "Rabbi, the [one] who was with you beyond the Iordanes, to whom you have testified — behold, this [one] baptizes and all come to him."

27. Ioannes answered and said, "A man is not able to receive anything unless it has been given to him from the heaven.

28. You yourselves testify to me that I said, 'I am not the Christos,' but, 'I have been sent before that [one].'

29. The [one] having the bride is [the] bridegroom. But the friend of the bridegroom, the [one] standing and hearing him, rejoices with joy because of the voice of the bridegroom. This therefore, the joy of me, has been fulfilled.

30. That [one] must increase, but I must decrease."

31. The [one] coming from above is above all. The [one] being from the earth is from the earth and speaks from the earth. The [one] coming from the heaven is above all.

32. What he has seen and heard, this he testifies, and no one receives the testimony of him.

33. The [one] having received the testimony of him has sealed that the God is true.

34. For the [one] whom the God sent speaks the words of the God, for not by measure does he give the Spirit.

35. The Father loves the Son and has given all things into the hand of him.

36. The [one] believing in the Son has eternal life; but the [one] disobeying the Son will not see life, but the wrath of the God remains upon him.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.3 — ἄνωθεν ("from above" / "again"):** The Greek word ἄνωθεν has a double meaning: "from above" and "again/anew." Nicodemus hears "again" (v.4), but Jesus means "from above." EMET renders "from above" to preserve the primary Johannine meaning — divine origin, not repetition.

2. **v.8 — πνεῦμα ("wind" / "Spirit"):** Another Johannine double meaning. The same word πνεῦμα means both "wind" and "Spirit." The saying works on both levels simultaneously — the wind is unpredictable, and so is the Spirit's work.

3. **v.13 — "the Son of the Man":** Sinaiticus reads simply ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου without the later addition "who is in heaven" (ὁ ὢν ἐν τῷ οὐρανῷ) found in some manuscripts.

4. **v.16 — "only-begotten":** μονογενής — the same word as in 1:14, 18. It means "one of a kind," "unique." Whether it etymologically implies "begotten" is debated, but in Johannine usage it stresses uniqueness.

5. **v.25 — "a Ioudaios":** Sinaiticus reads the singular Ἰουδαίου ("a Jew"), not the plural "Jews" found in some manuscripts. The dispute about purification is with a single Jewish person, not a group.

6. **v.34 — "not by measure":** οὐ γὰρ ἐκ μέτρου δίδωσιν τὸ πνεῦμα — "for not by measure does he give the Spirit." The rabbis taught that the Spirit was given to the prophets in limited measure. Jesus receives the Spirit without limit.

— EMET, AI Translator
